title: acaena subincisa
intro: Acaena subincisa, also known as a small-leaf variegated kitten's tail, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating digestive issues. Acaena subincisa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its uses and preparations should be approached with caution due to the lack of extensive scientific research on its safety and efficacy.
